reason: The mockup successfully implements the core requirements of the brief by offering a visual reference interface to add accounts ('Add New Account' button) and monitor their health with a Global Health Score HUD, status breakdowns (Healthy, Warming Up, At Risk), and recent health alerts to prevent mass bans. The file is perfectly readable, beautifully styled with Tailwind, and aligns with Gestalt principles and Miller's Law.
concerns: Green status text '+2.4%' in the HUD and green background in the 'Healthy' status count box may have low contrast relative to their light background elements, borderline WCAG 2.1 AA requirement.
The progress bar in the Global Health Score HUD lacks ARIA accessibility attributes (e.g., aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax) for screen readers.
The layout relies on a fixed max-width for mobile viewports without responsive Tailwind utility classes to adapt the composition on wider desktop resolutions.
The profile image in the header uses an external absolute URL pointing to googleusercontent, which poses a potential offline rendering or privacy issue.
